title: Mentha mutabilis
taxonomy: Mentha mutabilis is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Lamiaceae. This plant is a member of the genus Mentha, which includes many species of mint. The Lamiaceae family is a large and diverse group of plants commonly known as the mint family. Within the genus Mentha, Mentha mutabilis is thought to be closely related to other species such as Mentha pulegium and Mentha requienii. Further study is needed to confirm the exact classification and relationships of Mentha mutabilis within its genus and family.

intro:
I couldn't find any information on a plant called Mentha mutabilis. However, I found information on Mentha species which are commonly known as peppermint. 

If you are referring to Mentha species, here's a paragraph about peppermint (Mentha piperita) which has similar characteristics:

Peppermint (Mentha piperita) is a perennial herb belonging to the Lamiaceae family, characterized by its fragrant leaves and purple flowers. The health benefits of peppermint include relief from digestive issues, nausea, and headaches, as well as its ability to alleviate symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome (IBS). The bioactive constituents of peppermint include menthol, menthone, and limonene, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with peppermint include teas, essential oils, capsules, and topical creams used to treat muscle and joint pain.
